bio.photo_notes: Portrait of Gertrude Virginia Young Winger (1926-2014).

burial.plot_notes: Grove Hill Cemetery, Oil City Venango County PA — with husband William Burton Winger.

marriages.spouse_research_notes: William Burton Winger (I00680, 1922-2012). Five children per Gertrude's 2014 obit: Michael Winger (Lakewood CO), Luann Valdesalice (Franklin PA), Laura Winger (Spring Lake NC), Gerald Winger (Gaston SC), Amy Liddle (Rochester NY). Seven grandchildren also named in obit.

career.notes: Employed many years as a nurse, retiring from Franklin Hospital. Multiple Methodist church memberships across Venango County and Chautauqua County NY.

narrative_blocks.header: Same Valentine's Day birthday as uncle Ransom Unger
narrative_blocks.body: Gertrude was born February 14, 1926 in Meadville Pennsylvania — sharing a Valentine's Day birthday with her maternal uncle Ransom Andrew Unger, born exactly thirty-eight years earlier on February 14, 1888. Both members of the same extended Crawford County Ellis-Unger family separated by a generation. Her mother Bertha would have known the family date.

narrative_blocks.header: Wedding date was tenth anniversary of Pearl Harbor
narrative_blocks.body: Gertrude married William Burton Winger on December 7, 1951 at Cochranton Pennsylvania — the ten-year anniversary of the Pearl Harbor attack. She was twenty-five, he was twenty-nine. They celebrated their sixtieth wedding anniversary in 2011, the year before his death.

narrative_blocks.header: Last surviving among eleven predeceased siblings
narrative_blocks.body: When Gertrude died in January 2014, she had outlived eleven documented half-siblings — nine Young, Stanley, and Free half-siblings from her mother's second marriage, plus Chester, Irvin, and Wilbur Lybarger from her mother's first marriage. Her own marriage to William Winger lasted sixty years until his June 2012 death, and she followed him eighteen months later.

burial.plot_notes: Grove Hill Cemetery, Oil City Venango County PA — with wife Gertrude.

career.notes: Forty years in the shipping department of Pennzoil Refinery at Rouseville, retiring in 1986. Hobbies: lawn care + landscape, woodworking, H-O scale model railroads.

narrative_blocks.header: Born Oil City, forty years at Pennzoil Refinery
narrative_blocks.body: William Winger was born February 6, 1922 in Oil City Pennsylvania — the same Venango County oil region where his wife's great-grandmother Florence Hannah Ellis had been born at Pithole fifty-eight years earlier. He spent forty years at Pennzoil Refinery's shipping department in Rouseville, retiring in 1986. The Pennsylvania oil industry that had briefly turned Pithole into a fifteen-thousand-person boomtown in 1865 became the family livelihood three generations later.

narrative_blocks.header: Sixty year marriage, hobbyist woodworker and railroader
narrative_blocks.body: William married Gertrude Virginia Young on December 7, 1951 — the tenth anniversary of Pearl Harbor. They celebrated their sixtieth anniversary in 2011 and he died the following year on June 29, 2012 at ninety. He enjoyed lawn care, woodworking, and H-O scale model railroads in retirement.

burial.plot_notes: Greendale Cemetery, Meadville Crawford County PA — body returned from Florida.

marriages.spouse_research_notes: Freeman Delbert Lybarger (I00671, 1874 MN - 1913 PA). Two confirmed sons: Freeman Wilbur Lybarger (1906-1988, WWII vet), Irvin Clarence Lybarger (1908-1973). Per Gertrude Winger's 2014 obit there may have been additional siblings: 'Chester, Irvin, and Wilbur Lybarger' — Chester (and possibly others) to be researched. Children built later stages.

Silas Howard Young (I00672, 1873-1953). Multiple children — confirmed Gertrude Virginia Young Winger (1926-2014); per Gertrude's obit also Joseph, Noble, Leah, Austin, Reno, David Young, Lois Stanley, Paul Young, Dortha Free. Children to be built later stages.

narrative_blocks.header: Twice widowed, ended life far from home
narrative_blocks.body: Bertha Mae Unger was widowed twice — first when Freeman Delbert Lybarger died in 1913 at thirty-nine, then when second husband Silas Howard Young died in 1953 after twenty-eight years of marriage. She moved south to Florida in her later years and died in Hollywood Broward County in 1959 at seventy-four. Her body was returned to Meadville and buried at Greendale Cemetery.

narrative_blocks.header: Two husbands, many children across both marriages
narrative_blocks.body: Bertha's first marriage to Freeman Lybarger produced at least two confirmed sons before his 1913 death — Freeman Wilbur (1906) and Irvin Clarence (1908). Her second marriage to Silas Young in 1925 produced multiple Young children including Gertrude Virginia born 1926. Per a later family obituary at least ten siblings spanned the two marriages — many remain unbuilt pending research.

burial.plot_notes: Greendale Cemetery, Meadville Crawford County PA — with father-in-law John Ellis and wife Florence Hannah Ellis Unger McFarland.

narrative_blocks.header: Minnesota-born settler died Pennsylvania at thirty-six
narrative_blocks.body: Harmon Demmington Unger was born March 24, 1857 in Minnesota — far from his eventual Pennsylvania home — and migrated east to Crawford County, where he married Florence Hannah Ellis in 1881. They had four children between 1882 and 1889. He died in Pennsylvania on January 29, 1894 at thirty-six, leaving Florence widowed with four children aged five to twelve.

burial.plot_notes: Greendale Cemetery, Meadville Crawford County PA — with father John Ellis and first husband Harmon Unger.

marriages.spouse_research_notes: Harmon Demmington Unger (I00668, 1857 MN - 1894 PA) — four children born to this marriage: Rush John Unger (1882), Bertha Mae Unger Young (1884), Ransom Andrew Unger (1888), Irvin Clarence Unger (1889). Children to be built Stage 6Y-91.

naming_inspiration.description: Middle name Hannah honors her paternal grandmother Hannah Ellis Ellis (1812-1876), who was alive at her birth and likely her godmother.

narrative_blocks.header: Born in Pennsylvania's most famous oil ghost town
narrative_blocks.body: Florence Hannah Ellis was born July 12, 1864 at Pithole City Venango County Pennsylvania — six months before oil was struck at the nearby Frazier Well in January 1865. The strike turned her birthplace into a boomtown of fifteen thousand with fifty hotels and the third-busiest post office in Pennsylvania, only to collapse by 1870 when the wells ran dry. By the time she could remember it, Pithole was already a ghost town.

narrative_blocks.header: Lost mother at seven, twice widowed by fifty
narrative_blocks.body: Florence's mother Lois Olive Hibbard died at twenty-four in 1871, five days before Florence's seventh birthday. Her father remarried and the second wife Elsie Ann Jolly also died young, leaving Florence's year-old half-brother John Clarence motherless in 1885. Florence herself would be twice widowed by age fifty — first husband Harmon Unger died at thirty-six in 1894, second husband Reuben Bishop McFarland outlived her by five years until 1938.

narrative_blocks.header: Married at seventeen, four children by thirty
narrative_blocks.body: Florence married Harmon Demmington Unger in 1881 at age seventeen — a Minnesota-born settler who had migrated to Crawford County Pennsylvania. They had four children between 1882 and 1889: Rush John, Bertha Mae, Ransom Andrew, and Irvin Clarence. When Harmon died in 1894 at thirty-six, she was thirty with four children aged five to twelve.

burialCemetery.name: Grove Hill Cemetery
burialCemetery.notes: Grove Hill Cemetery, Oil City Venango County PA. Burial site of Gertrude Virginia Young Winger (HD4288, gen 10 Hooker descendant) and her husband William Burton Winger — both buried in Venango County's Oil City, the same county where Florence Hannah Ellis was born at Pithole City in 1864.
